title: cyperus pallidus
intro: Cyperus pallidus, also known as the pale papyrus or umbrella sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Cyperus pallidus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's rhizome is often dried and used in teas or infusions, while its essential oil is used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
